## Phases

- [ ] Phase 1 — connect-and-core-dashboard
- [ ] Phase 2 — filtered-charts
- [ ] Phase 3 — insights-and-sessions
- [ ] Phase 4 — history-and-export
- [ ] Phase 5 — two-account-tracking
- [ ] Phase 6 — tips-tab-and-final-polish

---

### Phase 1 — connect-and-core-dashboard

**Goal:** User opens the file, picks `~/.claude/projects`, and gets the one-glance answer "should I open Claude Code right now?" plus window/cap/today stats and the week forecast.
**Scope:** Single `burnboard.html`. Design system (sections 4.1–4.6: all CSS vars, card/button styles, orange glow, film grain, scrollbar, motion keyframes). Boot logic happy path (5.1: no handle → Connect; handle + permission → Sync → Dashboard). Connect screen (5.2) and Sync screen (5.4). Nav + tab bar shell with dashboard tab active (section 6). Settings overlay (section 12) — plan, billing start day, timezone, save/wipe. IndexedDB layer (13.1 schema v2: kv, turns, sessions, windows, monthly_cache; openDB/put/get/getAll/clear). Folder picker + JSONL parsing (section 14: recursive walk, incremental skip by lastModified, parseJsonl, buildSessions, buildWindows, write to IDB, save dirHandle). Computation for Start Check (7.1 state machine), Mini Stats (7.2), Week Forecast (7.5) using 15.2/15.3 cap + window math and section 17 caps.
**Depends on:** None
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Opening the file with no IDB data shows the Connect screen with tagline, trust strip, accordion, numbered steps, and the `select ~/.claude/projects` CTA.
- [ ] Picking a folder runs the Sync screen with live status messages and a progress bar, then lands on the Dashboard.
- [ ] Turns are parsed per section 14 rules (only `type === "assistant"`, skip zero-token and missing-id lines) and written to the `turns`, `sessions`, and `windows` stores.
- [ ] Start Check renders the correct one of the six states (good / caution_peak / caution_budget / danger / weekend / no_data) with matching border color, headline, body, and the mono window-remaining clock.
- [ ] Mini Stats row shows Current Window, Weekly Cap, and Today vs Average with the specified color thresholds and tooltips.
- [ ] Week Forecast shows the severity-colored sentence, opus/sonnet progress bars, and the community-estimate disclaimer.
- [ ] Settings overlay saves plan/billing-day/timezone to the kv store and re-renders; Wipe all data confirms then returns to Connect.
- [ ] Numbers use JetBrains Mono; reload reconnects from the saved handle without re-picking (permission-granted path).
**Ship with:** `/ship implement phase 1: connect-and-core-dashboard`

---

### Phase 2 — filtered-charts

**Goal:** User can filter by range and model and see daily burn, when-they-work heatmap, model split, and top projects update accordingly.
**Scope:** `burnboard.html`. Filter bar (7.4: range 7d/30d/90d/all, model all/opus/sonnet/haiku) wired to re-query IDB and re-render the affected sections only. Daily Burn bar chart (7.6, Chart.js). Peak Hour Heatmap (7.7, CSS grid 7×24, log intensity, peak tint, local-TZ peak note via `Intl.DateTimeFormat`). Model Breakdown doughnut (7.8 left, Chart.js). Top Projects horizontal bar (7.8 right). Builds the corresponding fields of the `d` object (15.1: daily_usage, heatmap, model_breakdown, top_projects).
**Depends on:** Phase 1 — connect-and-core-dashboard
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Changing range or model re-renders the four sections in this phase but leaves Start Check, Mini Stats, and Forecast unchanged (per 7.4).
- [ ] Daily burn chart shows token bars per day in range, highlights today, and shows the empty state when the range has no usage.
- [ ] Heatmap renders 7×24 cells colored by token intensity, tints weekday peak columns (13–18 UTC), shows the local-timezone peak-hours note, and shows the "add more data" state under 3 days of data.
- [ ] Model breakdown doughnut shows opus/sonnet/haiku/unknown segments with center total and legend; top projects shows up to 8 folders by token volume.
- [ ] All four respect the active filter selection.
**Ship with:** `/ship implement phase 2: filtered-charts`

---

### Phase 3 — insights-and-sessions

**Goal:** User sees plain-language insights about wasteful patterns with copyable fixes, and can drill into recent sessions turn-by-turn plus see cost/summary totals.
**Scope:** `burnboard.html`. Smart Insights (7.3: all four triggers — session spiral, cache alert, peak penalty, opus waste — severity ordering, max 3, default green card, copy buttons with the 1500ms revert from 4.5). Sessions table (7.9: last 20 sessions, columns, relative time, row expand to per-turn detail from IDB by session_id, context-growth mini-bar, heavy-context highlighting, one open at a time). Cost + Summary grid (7.10, with API-pricing note from section 16). Builds `d.insights`, `d.recent_sessions`, `d.turns_by_session`, `d.cost_by_model`, `d.summary`.
**Depends on:** Phase 2 — filtered-charts
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Each of the four insight triggers fires on data matching its condition and stays silent otherwise; when none fire the single green "nothing alarming" card shows.
- [ ] When more than three insights qualify, only three render in danger > warning > info priority order.
- [ ] Each insight card has the correct severity color and a working copy button that shows "copied!" then reverts after 1500ms.
- [ ] Sessions table lists recent sessions with project, relative when, duration, short model name, turns, and formatted tokens; clicking a row expands per-turn detail and collapses the previously open row.
- [ ] Expanded rows show the context-growth mini-bar and mark turns whose input exceeds 3× turn 1 as "heavy context".
- [ ] Cost-by-model and Summary tables compute from filtered data and show the API-pricing-equivalent note.
**Ship with:** `/ship implement phase 3: insights-and-sessions`

---

### Phase 4 — history-and-export

**Goal:** User can switch to the History tab and review usage over time by month, week, and billing cycle, then export it as CSV.
**Scope:** `burnboard.html`. History tab (section 8). Monthly view (8.1: 12-card grid + comparison chart) backed by `recomputeMonthlyCache()` run post-sync (15.4) and the `monthly_cache` store. Weekly view (8.2: 12-week table + sparkline) via `getWeeklyBuckets()` (15.5). Billing cycle view (8.3) using `billing_start_day` from settings. Export CSV (8.4: client-side Blob download).
**Depends on:** Phase 1 — connect-and-core-dashboard
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] History tab opens with Monthly / Weekly / Billing Cycle toggle and renders monthly cards newest-first with token totals, sessions, active days, dominant model, and vs-prior delta.
- [ ] `recomputeMonthlyCache()` runs after sync and writes per-month records; the monthly comparison chart and rolling-average overlay render from them.
- [ ] Weekly view shows the last 12 Mon–Sun weeks (zero-activity weeks faded, oldest week shows "—") plus the sparkline.
- [ ] Billing cycle view uses `billing_start_day` to show the current cycle card and last-3-cycles table.
- [ ] Export CSV downloads `burnboard-history-YYYY-MM-DD.csv` with the specified columns, fully client-side.
**Ship with:** `/ship implement phase 4: history-and-export`

---

### Phase 5 — two-account-tracking

**Goal:** A user with two Claude accounts can tag each sync and split History totals by account.
**Scope:** `burnboard.html`. Section 10. Account 1/2 name fields already present in settings (Phase 1) — activate two-account mode when Account 2 is filled. Sync prompt modal before sync (10.2) writing `account_label` on all turns/sessions for that sync. Account selector in History tab (10.3). Combined totals card at top of History (10.3). Per-account `monthly_cache` (account_label dimension). Error-table behaviors for account edge cases (section 18: prompt dismissed → Primary; Account 2 cleared → revert to single-account, nothing lost).
**Depends on:** Phase 4 — history-and-export
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] With Account 2 empty, no account UI appears anywhere (single-account users see nothing new).
- [ ] With Account 2 named, clicking sync shows the account prompt; the chosen label is written to every turn from that sync, and "Both / Unsure" tags as "combined".
- [ ] History tab shows the account selector (All / Primary / Alt) and filters all history views to the selection.
- [ ] The combined-totals card shows per-account and combined tokens/sessions.
- [ ] Dashboard tab (Start Check, Forecast, Mini Stats) keeps using all data regardless of account label.
- [ ] Clearing Account 2 reverts the UI to single-account view without losing already-tagged turns.
**Ship with:** `/ship implement phase 5: two-account-tracking`

---

### Phase 6 — tips-tab-and-final-polish

**Goal:** User gets the six efficiency tips, a smooth one-click reconnect on browser restart, the "what's coming" teaser, and all the empty/error/accessibility finishing touches.
**Scope:** `burnboard.html`. Token Tips tab (section 9: six cards, copy buttons, personalisation badges after 7+ days of data). Reconnect screen + boot revoked-permission branch (5.1 / 5.3: `requestPermission()` on stored handle, skip-to-last-data link). What's Coming tab/modal (section 11: static animated marketing panel, feature pills, email capture UI only). Toast system (e.g. "all caught up ✓", "skipped N malformed lines"). Favicon update by Start Check state. All remaining empty states and the section 18 error-handling table (browser detection bar, AbortError swallow, malformed-line count, permission-denied skip, mid-sync failure retry, stale-cache note). `prefers-reduced-motion` honored across all animations (4.6).
**Depends on:** Phase 5 — two-account-tracking
**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Token Tips tab shows all six cards with saving-badge pills and working copy buttons; personalisation badges replace generic text once 7+ days of data exist.
- [ ] After a browser restart with a saved handle but revoked permission, the Reconnect screen appears and its CTA reconnects in one click without a new picker; the skip link loads the dashboard from IDB.
- [ ] What's Coming tab renders the static teaser with feature pills and email-capture UI (no network calls).
- [ ] Toasts fire for "all caught up ✓" (0 new turns) and malformed-line counts; favicon reflects the current Start Check state.
- [ ] Firefox/Safari shows the amber browser-support bar with the folder button disabled; cancelling the picker is swallowed silently; mid-sync failure shows a retry and falls back to existing IDB data.
- [ ] With `prefers-reduced-motion` set, all entrance/stagger/counter animations are disabled.
**Ship with:** `/ship implement phase 6: tips-tab-and-final-polish`
